activation_results.reason: Input matches the description verbatim — canonical trigger.

criterion_results.text: Australian English used throughout the narrative (colour, optimise, behaviour, organise)
criterion_results.evidence: Uses 'colour', 'Colours' in section headings and body throughout; no American spellings observed.

criterion_results.text: Output is machine-readable where the description implies (valid JSON/SQL/YAML)
criterion_results.evidence: HEX/RGB/HSL/OKLCH values rendered as parseable code spans (e.g. `oklch(0.27 0.07 250)`, `rgb(10, 37, 64)`); palette and contrast tables are structured markdown.
Includes Palette Snapshot, Primary Colours, Secondary Colours, Semantic Colours, Neutral Ramp, and Contrast Validation — the expected canonical sections for a brand colour palette.

judge_result.notes: Artefact satisfies the three criteria: Australian English is consistent ('colour', 'Colours'), colour values are presented in machine-readable code spans with structured tables, and the document contains the canonical sections expected of a brand palette deliverable (snapshot, primary, secondary, semantic, neutral ramp, contrast validation with WCAG AA/AAA outcomes).
